text: In this dim hallway, bathed in hard artificial light, I am keenly aware of my own solitude. There is a sense of isolation—sterile, almost antiseptic—with only the silent glass and the coiled fire hose as witnesses. The angles and shadows make me feel slightly disoriented, heightening my sense of existential inquiry: what does this place expect from me? I feel both cautious and curious, as if on the threshold of discovery or retreat.
